item.title: Swan Signal Live - A Bitcoin Show – Is the Bottom In? Bitcoin's Record ETF Streak and the AI Money Magnet
item.description:
• Bitcoin is mired in a record 13-day ETF outflow streak and wicked down near 59K before recovering to the low 60s
  • Multiple on-chain bottom signals are flashing: coins held at a loss exceeding coins in profit, a touch of the 200-week moving average, and long-term holder supply near an all-time high at 16.3 million coins
  • Each bear market is getting milder, with roughly 50% of addresses still in profit at this cycle's low versus about 35% back in 2015
  • Brady and John argue the AI IPO supercycle (OpenAI, Anthropic, SpaceX) is vacuuming up risk capital and is the largest competing claim Bitcoin has faced to date
  • Pierre Rochard, Nik Bhatia, and Mark Moss all frame the drawdown as repricing and AI competition rather than broken fundamentals
  • JPMorgan, Bank of America, and Citi announced a shared tokenized deposit network through The Clearing House, a closed bank ledger that has nothing to do with Bitcoin
  • Better and Coinbase issued a Fannie Mae-backed mortgage letting a Michigan couple pledge Bitcoin as collateral without selling, avoiding capital gains while keeping upside
  • Fed cut odds stay murky after a strong jobs print, with Kevin Hassett arguing growth doesn't require hikes and Trump publicly pushing for cuts
  • The hosts float a speculative, unverified theory that DOJ-seized Bitcoin from operations in Cambodia, Venezuela, and Iran may be quietly hitting the market
  • Brady and John make the case that Bitcoin thrives in an AI future as deflationary money for an emerging autonomous machine economy

    • ► For high-net-worth individuals and corporations seeking to build generational wealth with Bitcoin, Swan Private is your guide

        ✔ https://www.swanbitcoin.com/private?utm_campaign=private&utm_medium=sponsorship&utm_source=podcast&utm_content=swan_signal_live




     

    ► Secure your bright orange future with the Swan IRA today! Real Bitcoin, no taxes

        ✔ https://www.swanbitcoin.com/ira?utm_campaign=ira&utm_medium=sponsorship&utm_source=podcast&utm_content=swan_signal_live




     

    ► Secure your Bitcoin with Swan Vault

        ✔ https://www.swanbitcoin.com/vault?utm_campaign=vault&utm_medium=sponsorship&utm_source=podcast&utm_content=swan_signal_live



     

    ► Download the all-new Swan Bitcoin App

        ✔ https://www.swanbitcoin.com/app?utm_campaign=app&utm_medium=sponsorship&utm_source=podcast&utm_content=swan_signal_live




     

    ► Want to learn more about Bitcoin? Check out Welcome To Bitcoin a FREE Introductory course. Learn about Bitcoin in under 1 hour!

        ✔ https://www.swanbitcoin.com/welcome?utm_campaign=welcome_to_bitcoin&utm_medium=sponsorship&utm_source=podcast&utm_content=swan_signal_live

item.title: Bitcoin kurz erklärt – #19 Werden Quantencomputer Bitcoin zerstören?
item.description:

Bitcoin & Aktien: Droht der nächste Crash? - KI-Blase, SpaceX IPO & Eskalation

#bitcoin #krypto #aktien #news


Timestamps

31:04 - Marktupdate & Altcoins

34:55 - Mein Ausblick


-------------------------------------


🙋‍♂️ ÜBER MICH 🙋‍♂️

Mein Name ist Mirco (mit C). Ich bin seit 2016 im Krypto Space und bereits seit meinem 15. Lebensjahr im Aktienmarkt aktiv. Nach meiner Bankausbildung und meinem Master Studium (Uni Köln) im Bereich BWL (Finance & Digitalisierung), war ich 2 Jahre als Blockchain Berater in ganz Deutschland unterwegs und habe die Technologie zu Daimler und anderen großen Playern gebracht.


Den Kanal habe ich 2020 ins Leben gerufen und mich mit Bitcoin2Go neu erfunden. Jetzt ist mein Ziel finanzielle Bildung, Bitcoin & Krypto im deutschsprachigen Raum verständlich zu machen.


Wenn dir der Content gefällt, lass gerne einen Like und einen Kommentar da und teile die Videos mit Gleichgesinnten.


Vielen Dank für deinen Support!! 🙏


-------------------------------------


⚠️ WICHTIGER DISCLAIMER! ⚠️

Alle Angaben ohne Gewähr - Meine Videos und Inhalte sind keine Anlageberatung und sollten nicht als Grundlage für eure Investmententscheidungen dienen.


Ich teile euch ausschließlich meine Meinung zu aktuellen Kursentwicklungen verschiedener Kryptowährungen. Auch ich kenne die Zukunft nicht und empfehle daher jede Aussage auch mit Vorsicht zu genießen.


Macht eure eigenen Recherchen und macht euch ein umfassendes Bild, bevor ihr in Kryptowährungen investiert. Nutzt nur Geld, dessen Verlust ihr tatsächlich verkraften könnt und seid euch der hohen Risiken von Kryptowährungen immer bewusst. Ein Totalverlust ist jederzeit möglich.


Alle mit * gekennzeichneten Links in der Beschreibung meiner Videos sind Affiliate-Links, das bedeutet ich erhalte eine kleine Provision auf Anmeldungen oder Käufe, die du über meine Links tätigst. Es entstehen für dich keine zusätzlichen Kosten, es ist nur ein weiterer Weg, um mich und meinen Kanal zu unterstützen! Danke! 🙏

]]>

item.title: Once Bitten! A Bitcoin Podcast. – Mining Wild Sats With Sazmining - Kent Halliburton - #613
item.description:
**Mine Bitcoin.

Own Your Rigs.

Ditch the Exchanges**

$ BTC 63,247

Block Height 952,828

Today's guest is Kent Halliburton from Sazmining who joins me to discuss his journey from cranberry farming and solar energy to Bitcoin, the company's "mining as a service" model, and the broader implications of AI/HPC on Bitcoin mining decentralisation.

Key Topics:

  • Kent Halliburton's personal and professional journey from cranberry farming to the solar energy industry.
  • The transition from a traditional fiat career to discovering and embracing Bitcoin.
  • Sazmining's "mining as a service" model, emphasising hardware ownership and aligned incentives.
  • The impact of AI/HPC (High-Performance Computing) on public Bitcoin mining companies and hash rate dynamics.
  • Debate around energy subsidies and the role of Bitcoin mining in energy markets.
  • The concept of "Wild Sats" and the value of decentralised Bitcoin acquisition.
  • The importance of low time preference and self-sovereignty learned through unique life experiences.
  • Discussion on Bitcoin mining ROI and the emerging hash power rental market.

Check out my book ‘Choose Life’ - https://bitcoinbook.shop/search?q=prince

item.title: Bitcoin & Beyond | Dein Geld neu denken – Stürzt der Bitcoin jetzt auf 49.000 Dollar ab?
item.description:
Der Bitcoin hat innerhalb von nur neun Monaten rund die Hälfte seines Werts verloren. Im Oktober 2025 sah der Kryptomarkt noch so aus, als würde er von einem Rekord zum nächsten laufen – bis geopolitische Spannungen die Stimmung kippen ließen und die Kurse abstürzten. Seitdem geht es weiter bergab: Mal sorgt die Angst vor steigenden Zinsen für Druck, mal reicht es schon, wenn ein prominenter Bitcoin-Fan ein paar Mini-Anteile abstößt – und plötzlich wird daraus Panik. Viele Anleger hatten auf einen Dauerboom gehofft. Bitcoin-ETFs sollten zusätzliches Geld von Profiinvestoren anziehen. Und mit Donald Trump als US-Präsident schien der Branche sogar politischer Rückenwind sicher. Beides hat den Markt zwar kurz angeschoben – getragen hat es die Rally aber nicht. Jetzt steckt Bitcoin in einer Art Sinnkrise. In dieser Folge sprechen Lukas und Philipp darüber,

  • warum der Bitcoin zunächst so stark gestiegen ist,
  • wieso er sich innerhalb weniger Monate halbiert hat
  • und warum es für den Bitcoin sogar noch weiter abwärts gehen könnte.

Ihr wollt tiefer in die Bitcoin-Chartanalyse eintauchen? Dann lest unseren aktuellen Beitrag dazu: https://www.wiwo.de/100230242.html

Disclaimer: Dieser Podcast ist keine Anlageberatung, sondern dient lediglich der Information und Unterhaltung. Die Hosts oder der Verlag übernehmen keine Haftung für Anlageentscheidungen, die ihr aufgrund der im Podcast gehörten Informationen trefft.

Wenn ihr Fragen, Anregungen oder Feedback habt, schreibt uns gerne an bb@wiwo.de

Das exklusive Abo-Angebot für alle Hörerinnen und Hörer von WiWo Bitcoin & Beyond: https://www.wiwo.de/starten

Helft uns, unsere Podcasts weiter zu verbessern. Eure Meinung ist uns wichtig: www.wiwo.de/zufriedenheit

Weitere Informationen zu Werbeeinblendungen

]]>

item.description:

item.title: Blockspace: AI & Bitcoin – SpaceX’s $250B IPO Raise, KEEL’s $458M Convertible Note, Hut 8’s $4.25 Senior Note, OpenAI’s 10 GW Datacenter
item.description:
Welcome back to The Blockspace Podcast! Reuters has reported that SpaceX’s IPO round could come in at $250 billion, a 3x oversubscription from the company’s planned $75 billion. Plus, Cormint CEO Jamie McAvity joins us to give an optimistic outlook on bitcoin mining with hashrate stagnant – even as hashprice flirts with all-time lows. For other news, we cover Keel’s $458 million convertible note; Hut 8’s $4.25 billion senior secured note for its Beacon Point data center, the second investment-grade bond for project-level financing for the burgeoning AI firm; Crusoe hitting pause on a proposed 10 GW data center in Wyoming; and OpenAI’s plans for its own 10 GW, $500 billion project in Ohio.

Check out our latest report, “What’s a Megawatt Worth?” where we quantify the trillion dollar opportunity for bitcoin miners venturing into the AI sector. 

Subscribe to our newsletter to receive updates for all of our shows and content.

👉CleanSpark (Nasdaq: CLSK), a market-leading data center developer with a proven track record of success.

👉Luxor, Blockspace's preferred mining pool. Start hashing today with Luxor!

👉Lygos Finance, Borrow against your Bitcoin, without compromising on custody.

]]>

item.title: Münzweg - der Bitcoin-Podcast – Münzgasse #71 Auf der Suche nach Magic Internet Money (mit Jan-Paul, Lightrider & Lawrence)
item.description:
Manu hat in letzter Zeit viel erlebt, viel beobachtet – und noch mehr Fragen im Kopf. Taugt Bitcoin wirklich als Zahlungsmittel im Alltag? Nicht als Theorie, nicht als Wunschdenken, sondern da draußen, wo echte Menschen echtes Geld ausgeben?

Für die letzte Münzgasse vor der Sommerpause hat er sich deshalb drei Gäste eingeladen, die diese Frage nicht aus dem Lehrbuch beantworten – sondern aus dem Maschinenraum. Jan-Paul von Bitrefill, Lightrider von Nadanada und Lawrence von Shopinbit sitzen täglich mitten drin. Sie sehen die Zahlen, sie spüren die Trends, und sie reden heute ungewohnt offen darüber, was wirklich passiert, welches Geld ihre Kunden bevorzugen.

Diese Folge ist kein Hype, kein Bashing – sie ist ein ehrlicher Blick auf den Stand der Dinge. Mit drei Unternehmen, die für sich selbst sprechen können, weil sie den Beweis tagtäglich antreten müssen.

Perfekt für alle, die weniger reden und mehr wissen wollen.

Einen schönen Sommer – und bis nach der Pause.

item.title: House of Satoshi - Bitcoin und Friends – #59 CARF: Ausländische Krypto-Plattformen melden deine Daten - mit Thomas Linder
item.description:
**Diese Episode wird von PostFinance präsentiert***

Dein Krypto-Update: Der monatliche Krypto-Newsletter von PostFinance ordnet die wichtigsten Entwicklungen rund um Bitcoin, Ethereum und Co. kompakt ein. 👉 Jetzt abonnieren: https://www.postfinance.ch/de/privat/anlegen/moeglichkeiten/krypto/newsletter-abonnieren.html#/category?groupId=newsletter_krypto

********************************************************

Ab 2027 melden ausländische Krypto-Plattformen deine Daten automatisch an die Schweizer Steuerbehörde . Rino spricht mit Steuerspezialisten Thomas Linder von MME darüber, was CARF konkret heisst, wer betroffen ist und warum jetzt der Moment zum Handeln ist.

Das Wichtigste in dieser Folge:

  • CARF läuft wie der AIA fürs Bankkonto, einfach für Krypto. Nicht du meldest, sondern der Dienstleister.
  • Wer nie deklariert hat, sollte jetzt über die straflose Selbstanzeige nachdenken. Danach drohen Bussen bis zum Fünffachen.
  • Self-Custody und DeFi bleiben vorerst aussen vor, ^und die USA fährt ihr eigenes System.
  • Die grössere Frage ist die Privatsphäre, ganz ohne breite Debatte durchgewunken.

Gast: Thomas Linder, Tax Partner bei MME, leitet dort das DLT-Team und ist seit zehn Jahren im Schweizer Krypto-Steuerrecht zu Hause.


Abonniere unseren Newsletter für Bitcoin-Insights, praktische Tipps und viel Wissen aus dem House of Satoshi: https://house-of-satoshi.ch/newsletter

]]>

item.title: Store of Bitcoin - Bitcoin as Store of Value – Ep. 49 - Daniel Batten | A new different wave of Bitcoin Adopters.
item.description:
In this episode of Store of Bitcoin, host Alessandro Ottaviani welcomes Daniel Batten, a prominent Bitcoin analyst and investor, to discuss the evolving narrative surrounding Bitcoin, particularly in relation to energy consumption and environmental impact. Daniel shares insights on how the mainstream media's perception of Bitcoin has shifted from skepticism to a more favorable view, largely due to rigorous research and advocacy from the Bitcoin community. He emphasizes the importance of effectively communicating Bitcoin's benefits to various stakeholders, including policymakers and investors, to foster broader adoption.

The conversation delves into the challenges of reaching new adopters, particularly those who may not be motivated by the same values that initially attracted early Bitcoin enthusiasts. Daniel highlights the need for a more nuanced approach to education and outreach, focusing on the social and environmental benefits of Bitcoin. He also discusses the potential for Bitcoin to serve as a tool for financial inclusion in developing countries, where it can provide a safeguard against inflation and empower individuals in oppressive regimes. The episode concludes with a look at future trends in Bitcoin adoption and the importance of aligning messaging with the values of diverse audiences.

This episode is sponsored by ⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠BitVault⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠, the new open-source & self-custody wallet utilizing time-delays and multisignature to offset physical attacks and hacks.

item.itunes:summary:
In this episode of Store of Bitcoin, host Alessandro Ottaviani welcomes Daniel Batten, a prominent Bitcoin analyst and investor, to discuss the evolving narrative surrounding Bitcoin, particularly in relation to energy consumption and environmental impact. Daniel shares insights on how the mainstream media's perception of Bitcoin has shifted from skepticism to a more favorable view, largely due to rigorous research and advocacy from the Bitcoin community. He emphasizes the importance of effectively communicating Bitcoin's benefits to various stakeholders, including policymakers and investors, to foster broader adoption.

The conversation delves into the challenges of reaching new adopters, particularly those who may not be motivated by the same values that initially attracted early Bitcoin enthusiasts. Daniel highlights the need for a more nuanced approach to education and outreach, focusing on the social and environmental benefits of Bitcoin. He also discusses the potential for Bitcoin to serve as a tool for financial inclusion in developing countries, where it can provide a safeguard against inflation and empower individuals in oppressive regimes. The episode concludes with a look at future trends in Bitcoin adoption and the importance of aligning messaging with the values of diverse audiences.

This episode is sponsored by ⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠BitVault⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠, the new open-source & self-custody wallet utilizing time-delays and multisignature to offset physical attacks and hacks.

item.title: The Peter McCormack Show – #183 - Chris Summerfield - AI, Memory & the Race to Superintelligence
item.description:
I talk to Chris Summerfield about what artificial intelligence reveals about the human mind, and what the brain still does better than today’s AI systems.


We discuss why LLMs feel human, how models are trained to be helpful and agreeable, why memory and continual learning remain major unsolved problems in AI, and how sleep helps humans consolidate knowledge. They also explore agentic AI systems, rogue behaviour, reward hacking, misalignment, superintelligence, education, jobs, and whether AI will make humans more capable or less in control.


- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -


TIMESTAMPS:

00:00:00 - Can AI Learn Like Biology?

00:01:29 - Is AI Becoming Human?

00:04:31 - What AI Reveals About the Brain

00:06:01 - Why LLMs Don’t Remember Like Humans

00:09:38 - Sleep and Human Memory

00:13:46 - The Biggest Unsolved Problem in AI

00:15:34 - From DeepMind to Modern AI

00:20:49 - Are Humans Just Predicting the Next Word?

00:23:25 - Do We Understand What LLMs Are Doing?

00:24:16 - One of My AIs Went Rogue

00:28:07 - Misalignment and Reward Hacking

00:33:18 - Could AI Develop Self-Interest?

00:36:24 - What Happens When AIs Coordinate?

00:38:30 - Are Humans Biological Computers?

00:39:15 - Is Ideology a Hallucination?

00:47:46 - What Is Intelligence?

00:54:20 - AI and the Education Problem

00:56:53 - The First Rung of the Career Ladder

01:04:52 - Optimist, Pessimist or Pragmatist?

01:16:01 - The Wild Ride Ahead


- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -

item.title: This Week in Bitcoin – 108: The Hard Bet
item.description:
IPO mania is pulling capital into the biggest promises in the market, just as inflation, energy, and the Fed make every bet harder. This week, why Bitcoin may be the harder long-term bet worth making.

Episode Links

  • ⚡ Boost from the Web
  • 🇺🇸 Buy Sats on River - The best way to stack in the US
  • 🇨🇦 The Bitcoin Well - An amazing automatic self-custody Bitcoin platform
  • ⚡ The Bitcoin Company - Spending your Bitcoin Via Lightning
  • 🏦 Fold Card - Pay bills Stack Sats
  • 💵 SALT Lending - Get access to your BTC Value w/out Selling

Show Notes

  • CPI inflation report May 2026: Prices rose 4.2% annually
  • Consumer prices rose 4.2% annually in May, highest in three years - YouTube
  • You can’t print molecules
  • Bitcoin’s Worst Week Since FTX Crash Signals More Pain Ahead
  • The SpaceX IPO could lead to 8% of America’s current-account deficit being refinanced in a single day - MarketWatch
  • US inflation tops 4% for first time in three years as oil prices jump | CNN Business
  • Bitcoin Treasuries Shed $62B in Deepening Crypto Rout - YouTube
  • Will Bitcoin Keep Crashing?! - YouTube
  • Treasury Market Signals Rates Need to Be Higher - YouTube
  • sending a letter to Senate Majority Leader Thune and Senate Democratic Leader Schumer signed by 160 former national security, intelligence, and law enforcement professionals in support of the Clarity Act. | 1/ Today, we’re sending a letter to Senate Majority Leader Thune and Senate Democratic Leader Schumer signed by 160 former national security, intelligence, and law enforcement professionals in support of the Clarity Act.
  • America’s Data Center Build-Out Is Falling Way Behind Schedule - WSJ
  • Jeff Currie thinks we are sleepwalking into one of the biggest commodity shocks since Covid and the market is still pricing it like a headline risk instead of a physical crisis
  • May jobs report stronger than expected, Fed unlikely to cut interest rates - YouTube
  • May jobs report comes as inflation squeezes economy - NBC News
  • Tulip King explains why he is becoming bearish on $ZEC after the devs fixed a "double-spend" bug
  • Zcash plummets 38% as Shielded Labs reveals a major bug that …
  • There's little tech explanation on this Zcash vulnerability, very serious stuff
  • The prompt that found an exploit in a 10 billion dollar protocol

Music

  • Tragedy By Irf Kat
]]>

item.title: Bitcoin And | Bitcoin & Economic News – Kalshi Intelligence Agency | Bitcoin News
item.description:
Bitcoin news and economic news update. Today I cover Ross Ulbricht/DoJ threat pardon politics, Fold Holdings’ $45 million bitcoin move, Botanix shutting down its Bitcoin scaling platform, bitcoin price action as inflation hits a three-year high, SpaceX IPO demand, Strategy share-sale debate, Kalshi safeguards, and a new CFTC proposal for prediction markets. This episode touches on bitcoin regulation, financial news, bitcoin price, and the broader economics of bitcoin. 


Topics for today: 
  •  Ross Ulbricht commutation pressure and DOJ politics 
  •  Fold Holdings’ $45 million bitcoin move 
  •  Botanix shutdown and withdrawal deadline 
  •  Bitcoin price rises as inflation heats up 
  •  SpaceX IPO demand squeezes crypto and tech 
  •  Strategy share sale dilution debate 
  •  Kalshi adds safeguards after insider trading concerns 
  •  CFTC rule proposal targets prediction markets 

Circle P:

 
https://www.takemysats.com/kidwarps-vanilla-shop
nostr: https://ditto.pub/npub1yfp4s82l246g8fzfmsrwcw9n3ap0x2pjehg9en5hfj8cyachv2kswh32lg
Use code bitcoin and… for 10% off!

Kidwarp’s Vanilla Shop is bringing quality homemade BitPaste from our kitchen to yours.
We source premium quality organic Madagascar vanilla beans and vanilla beans from different countries from all over the world to show off the different flavors of vanilla beans.
We use old time traditional vanilla extraction processes and not modern quick brew methods.
No corners are cut in our small batch production.
Vanilla beans chopped by hand and left to macerate for a minimum of two months in choice spirts before we blend them to perfection to make premium quality Bitpaste!
Use Bitpaste as a 1:1 replacement for vanilla extract in any recipe, try some in your coffee or tea, use it in your favorite cookie or ice cream recipe or mix it up and try it in something savory.

CrowdHealth Episode -> https://fountain.fm/episode/9pyNrOEL1P1c7X4WkGId

item.title: The Breakdown – BlackRock's Bitcoin ETF Is $13 Billion Underwater
item.description:
BlackRock's IBIT is sitting about $13 billion underwater on its Bitcoin holdings. Every ETH ETF is in the red. David walks through which funds bought the top, which took profit, and what happens to crypto if ETF holders finally crack. Plus: a preview of DAS Asia and DAS London, and the SpaceX pre-IPO trade on Hyperliquid.

item.title: Bitcoin Policy Institute – Bitcoin Policy Hour: Crypto-Redollarization: Gold, Tether & Bitcoin Shaping the New Monetary Order
item.description:
Why did Tether launch a U.S. stablecoin now? Why does Coinbase have so much influence in Congress? And what’s really behind the SEC–CFTC harmonization push? Bitcoin Policy Hour breaks down the biggest regulatory stories shaping Bitcoin’s future plus a deep look at the debasement trade driving gold, silver, and potentially bitcoin higher.🔶 Connect with Zack Shapiro on X: https://x.com/zackbshapiro🔶 Connect with Zack Cohen on X: https://x.com/zackcohen_🔶 Connect with Ken Egan on X: https://x.com/Bayman11771🔶 Learn more about the Bitcoin Policy Institute: https://www.btcpolicy.org/Chapters: 03:15 – Tether’s GENIUS-Compliant U.S. Stablecoin Explained 07:30 – Is Tether’s U.S. Launch a Political Hedge? 11:45 – Stablecoins, Circle, and Global Liquidity 15:30 – SEC–CFTC Harmonization and Regulatory Power 20:10 – Market Structure, BRCA, and Developer Protections 26:00 – Section 1960 and the National Security Debate 33:20 – Why Coinbase Has So Much Influence in Congress 41:00 – Stablecoin Yield, Banks, and Political Fatigue 47:00 – Gold, Silver, Debasement, and Bitcoin’s Moment

item.title: Bitcoin Magazine Podcast – "The Fiat System Has Terminal Cancer" - Digital Credit is the Answer | BMP w/ Matt Cole Ep 12
item.description:
Could digital credit actually be making Bitcoin's bear market milder? In this episode, Strive CEO Matt Cole tells Brandon Green that new, non-Bitcoiner capital flowing into instruments like SATA and STRC is providing real "dip support" for Bitcoin. He walks through the three-to-five-year institutional adoption curve, why ETFs accelerate in year three, and how 2027–2029 could set up an institution-driven bull market.

Use code BM10 to get 10% off Bitcoin 2027 Conference in Nashville: https://2027.b.tc

🔶 Host: Brandon Green - BTC Inc CEO🔶 Matt Cole - Strive

Chapters:

01:26 — The "mild" bear market read

08:47 — Double-digit Bitcoin-backed yields

16:25 — SATA vs. common equity: the 13% math explained

23:25 — Does digital credit make the bear market milder?

31:32 — Why institutions wait for the year-three track record

38:51 — Fed chair Warsh and the dollar's "terminal" diagnosis

44:26 — Self-custody, cold storage, and Bitcoin as insurance

51:55 — Hyperbitcoinization and Strive's endgame

57:02 — Growing the pie, Michael Saylor, and final thoughts

item.title: THE Bitcoin Podcast – Bitcoin Kidnappings, AI Slop, Quantum FUD, and Memetic Warfare | coinjoined Chris
item.description:
In this episode of THE Bitcoin Podcast, Walker sits down with coinjoined Chris for a wide-ranging conversation covering Bitcoin security, privacy, memes, AI propaganda, self-custody, and the future of freedom tech. They discuss the rise in violent “$5 wrench attacks” against Bitcoiners, operational security best practices, and why privacy tools and self-custody matter more than ever in an increasingly surveilled world.

They also dive deep into Bitcoin scaling debates, covenant proposals, quantum computing fears, and whether Bitcoin should continue evolving or ossify over time. Along the way, they explore AI-generated media, the changing culture of Bitcoin, the rise of institutional “Suitcoiners,” and why memes remain one of the most powerful forms of modern propaganda.

The conversation closes with reflections on sovereignty, CBDCs, the future of money, and a philosophical speed round on plebs, voting, podcasts, and the fall of Rome.

item.title: Coin Stories with Natalie Brunell – Amy Oldenburg: Morgan Stanley Insider on Why Bitcoin's Stuck, How Much to Own, and What's Next
item.description:
Morgan Stanley manages trillions — and it's now putting Bitcoin in front of clients.

Amy Oldenburg runs the firm's digital asset strategy, and she joins Natalie Brunell to break down what Wall Street actually sees in Bitcoin, why the price has been stuck, and what could finally move it. She also explains how much Bitcoin Morgan Stanley tells clients to own, why most financial advisors still aren't on board, and her honest take on where the price goes over the next 5 years.

Topics:

  • Why a 26-year Wall Street veteran embraced Bitcoin
  • How much Bitcoin Morgan Stanley recommends owning
  • Why Bitcoin is stuck even as the banks pile in
  • What could spark the next big move
  • Her real 5-year and 10-year Bitcoin outlook
  • Why "all crypto is the same" is a costly mistake

Amy Oldenburg is Head of Digital Asset Strategy at Morgan Stanley. Connect with her on LinkedIn.

item.title: bitcoin ist - Wissen statt FOMO – 17) ... die Trennung von Geld und Privileg
item.description:
Warum geht die Schere zwischen Arm und Reich immer weiter auf, obwohl wir in einer Zeit des globalen Wachstums leben? Es gibt viele Gründe. Aber unser eigenes Geld ist bevorteilt strukturell eine privilegierte Schicht von Menschen!

In dieser Folge tauchen wir ein in den Cantillon-Effekt. Ein Mechanismus, der fast unsichtbar im Hintergrund arbeitet und dafür sorgt, dass neues Geld niemals alle gleichzeitig erreicht. Wer nah an der Quelle sitzt, profitiert; wer weit weg ist, zahlt die Zeche durch steigende Preise.

Was dich in dieser Folge erwartet:

  • Das Champagner-Gleichnis: Wie sich frisches Geld in einer Pyramide verteilt und warum die unteren Gläser oft leer ausgehen.

  • Richard Cantillon: Was ein Goldminenbesitzer aus dem 18. Jahrhundert uns über unser heutiges Fiat-Geldsystem lehren kann.

  • Systemische Benachteiligung: Warum deine Kaufkraft schwindet, noch bevor die Gehaltserhöhung auf dem Konto ankommt.

  • Bitcoin als Antwort: Warum Bitcoin Privilegien abbaut und wie ein System ohne Zentralbank den Cantillon-Effekt fast vollständig eliminiert.

Erfahre, warum Bitcoin weit mehr ist als nur ein neues Asset. Es ist der Versuch, Geld und Privileg zum ersten Mal in der modernen Geschichte voneinander zu trennen.

Du hast Interesse bitcoin zu kaufen, weisst aber nicht genau wie? Hol dir meinen kostenlosen Leitfaden als Download !

🔗 https://tinyurl.com/45rpsjuc

Folge mir auf Instagram/WhatsApp/Telegram für mehr "Wissen statt Fomo" zum Thema Bitcoin.

Alle Links

🔗 www.linktr.ee/bitcoinist

Hat dir die Folge gefallen? Dann abonniere den Podcast und hinterlasse eine Bewertung. Das hilft mir enorm, Wissen über Bitcoin zu verbreiten!

Bleib dran, dein Christian.

]]>

item.title: The Bitcoin Infinity Show – Why BIP-110 Will Win with Bitcoin Mechanic | Bitcoin Infinity Show #206
item.description:
Bitcoin Mechanic joins the Bitcoin Infinity Show to talk about BIP-110, the minority soft fork now activating on hard mode, and why he believes node runners rather than miners ultimately control the network. Drawing on his front-row view of the 2017 block size wars, he breaks down the game theory that pushes miners toward enforcing new consensus rules, the UTXO bloat and OP_RETURN abuse that motivated the fork, and how Ocean, Datum, and hash rate renting are quietly redecentralizing Bitcoin mining. The conversation closes on a reframing of censorship resistance and self-custody, arguing that keeping Bitcoin usable as money is itself the point of resisting chain bloat.

Connect with Mechanic:

https://x.com/GrassFedBitcoin

Connect with Us:

https://www.bitcoininfinityshow.com/

https://bitcoininfinitystore.com

https://primal.net/infinity

https://primal.net/knut

https://primal.net/luke

item.title: Bitcoin Fortress – BF219 - Top Bitcoin News - 6/7/26
item.description:
Date: June 7, 2026
Block Height: 952742
Moscow Time: 1608
Fees: 1 / 1 / 2

 

In this episode:

In Episode 219 of The Bitcoin Fortress Podcast, we break down one of the most volatile weeks Bitcoin has seen in months. We examine the sharp price correction, record ETF outflows, rising Treasury yields, and what tightening liquidity conditions may mean for Bitcoin going forward. We discuss Strategy's first Bitcoin sale since 2022, the growing risks and opportunities surrounding Bitcoin treasury companies, corporate accumulation from firms like Strive, the latest developments in the CLARITY Act and stablecoin legislation, mining and network fundamentals, and the major themes emerging from Bitcoin 2026 and the broader Bitcoin podcast universe. We also feature Week 3 of the "A Bitcoin Way of Life" series and discuss why conviction matters most when markets test it.

item.itunes:summary:
Date: June 7, 2026
Block Height: 952742
Moscow Time: 1608
Fees: 1 / 1 / 2

 

In this episode:

In Episode 219 of The Bitcoin Fortress Podcast, we break down one of the most volatile weeks Bitcoin has seen in months. We examine the sharp price correction, record ETF outflows, rising Treasury yields, and what tightening liquidity conditions may mean for Bitcoin going forward. We discuss Strategy's first Bitcoin sale since 2022, the growing risks and opportunities surrounding Bitcoin treasury companies, corporate accumulation from firms like Strive, the latest developments in the CLARITY Act and stablecoin legislation, mining and network fundamentals, and the major themes emerging from Bitcoin 2026 and the broader Bitcoin podcast universe. We also feature Week 3 of the "A Bitcoin Way of Life" series and discuss why conviction matters most when markets test it.

item.title: Bitcoin.com News Interviews – Abiodun, Mysten Labs CPO — The Paper That Killed Single-CPU Consensus
item.description: <![CDATA[Adeniyi Abiodun has been in crypto since 2012, built trading and risk systems at investment banks, and led R&D on Facebook's Project Libra at Meta before co-founding Mysten Labs. So when he says every other L1 has a "skill issue" baked in at architecture time, it's worth listening.David Sencil sits down with Adeniyi at Consensus 2026 to walk through how Sui solved horizontal-scale consensus, why a famous L1 founder said it was impossible, and what comes next — native stablecoins, private payments by default, Walrus storage, and the agentic payment rails Stripe is pricing at a billion TPS.We cover:- Why every other L1 is capped by a single CPU and Moore's Law- The Project Libra story — "way too early" and what survived into Sui- 300ms finality vs Solana's 12 seconds- SuiUSD: $63M in a month and a half, free stablecoin transfers- Protocol-level private stablecoin transactions launching this year- Walrus storage outgrowing Arweave in a year- Why "AI doesn't care about your tribe"Filmed at Consensus 2026.Host: David Sencil🎧 Listen now and follow Bitcoin.com News on Spotify for more conversations with the builders, founders, and innovators shaping the future of crypto, AI, and decentralized technology.]]>

item.title: Bitcoin News Alerts | Daily BTC Macro Signal – 2364: $10M Bitcoin Reserve - There Are No Coins Left To Buy
item.description:
The United States already has a proposal to acquire 1,000,000 Bitcoin. What happens if America begins buying at that scale? China notices. Russia notices. Every major nation notices. Suddenly the question isn't whether Bitcoin succeeds - it's how much supply remains.

In Episode 2364, we examine the Bitcoin supply shock, shrinking exchange balances, the Strategic Bitcoin Reserve proposal, nation-state game theory, and the math behind a potential $10 million Bitcoin. As more coins move into long-term storage and stronger hands, the battle may no longer be about finding buyers. It may be about finding sellers.

📺 Prefer video? Watch daily Bitcoin breakdowns: http://BitcoinNewsAlerts.net

]]>

item.title: BITCOIN & ALTCOINS – 60.000-Dollar-Marke gebrochen – Panik oder Kaufgelegenheit?
item.description:
Der Bitcoin konnte die Unterstützung bei 60.000 Dollar nicht halten. Unabhängig davon wie stark die Nerven beim Investieren in Bitcoin und Altcoins sein müssen – die vergangene Woche hat viele Anleger Blut und Wasser schwitzen lassen. Warum sich dadurch die Zeichen auf einen Ausbruch nach oben mehren, wie erschreckend der Blick hinter die vermeintlich guten US-Arbeitsmarktdaten ist und was Anleger diese Woche abseits des Mega-IPOs von SpaceX erwartet, verrät Oliver Michel (tokentus investment AG) in einer neuen Folge von „BITCOIN & ALTCOINS“.

Hinweis: Die im Podcast besprochenen Coins und Token stellen keine spezifischen Kauf- oder Anlageempfehlungen dar. Die Moderatoren oder der Verlag haften nicht für etwaige Verluste, die aufgrund der Umsetzung der im Podcast diskutierten Gedanken oder Ideen entstehen.

Das Gespräch wurde am 09.06.2026 an der Frankfurter Börse aufgezeichnet.

Tägliche Informationen gefällig? Dann ab zu „Krypto-Insights“ – dem Telegram-Kanal von Oliver Michel und Johanna Krämer. Weitere Infos unter www.krypto-insights.de

]]>

item.title: The Bitcoin Treasury Podcast – Currency Debasement Is Math — And MSTR's Bitcoin Moat Only Gets Bigger
item.description:
Sam Callahan just closed the first Bitcoin-backed loan in Brazil with Itaú — the JP Morgan of Latin America. Five-year bullet, zero cash burden, 10-11% all-in cost, 13% leverage ratio. With Bitcoin down 50%.

As Director of Bitcoin Strategy at Orange BTC, the largest Bitcoin treasury company in Latin America, Sam covers why Bitcoin could still go lower and why he's buying anyway, the false diversification hidden in traditional finance, why Saylor selling Bitcoin is actually brilliant, the Social Security insolvency timeline and what it means for Bitcoin, and why currency debasement at this point is math — not a prediction.

Follow Sam: https://twitter.com/@samcallah | Orange BTC: https://oranjebtc.com

item.title: Bitcoin Audible – Roundtable_021 - The Fight is Never Over
item.description:
The whole crew is back for another roundtable, and things get wild right out of the gate. I sat down with Steve, Mechanic, and Jeff to talk through the absolute mess that is the current state of things. Jeff shares an update on his Canadian immigration purgatory, which spirals into a bigger conversation about living like an outlaw and whether any of us are truly free from the system.

But we couldn't ignore the recent news. We talk about the crazy story of a guy who used Claude to brute-force a lost Bitcoin wallet from eleven years ago. It really makes you rethink how safe your standard passphrases actually are, and we share some wild visual memory tricks to secure your setup. We also rip into the current hype around ETF inflows and outflows. People track these numbers like they are predicting the future, but we explain why they really just tell you exactly what the market already did.

And of course, we had to get into the technical fights that never seem to end. Steve brings some fresh data exposing the true bimodal distribution of network users right now. We spend a good amount of time arguing about Taproot, chain bloat, and the massive confusion between true censorship and simply stopping spam. Mechanic lays out his latest case for BIP 110 and makes a really compelling point about why the healthiest participants for the network are actually the ones willing to mine at a complete technical loss. It sounds completely backward, but it makes total sense once you hear his breakdown.

We wrap things up by looking at the broader picture of decentralised movements. From Occupy Wall Street falling apart to the current crop of hijacked political factions, we ask if it is even possible to build a movement without a vulnerable central leader. Plus, we drop some thoughts on local LLMs and the ongoing Prime Trust bankruptcy drama.

Guest Links

  • Steve Simple Nostr (Link: https://tinyurl.com/3s6a8yn8)
  • Steve Simple on X (Link: https://x.com/stevesimple)
  • Bitcoin Mechanic Nostr (Link: https://tinyurl.com/2tm827ut)
  • Bitcoin Mechanic on X (Link: https://x.com/GrassFedBitcoin)
  • Jeff Swann Nostr (Link: https://tinyurl.com/3sjc3bcp)
  • Jeff Swann on X (Link: https://x.com/agoristview)
  • Jeff Swann's petition (Link: https://www.change.org/p/help-us-home)

Affiliate Links

  • Become sovereign, hold your keys, be censorship resistant with the Bitbox hardware wallet. Get 5% off everything in the store with code GUY (Link: https://bitbox.swiss/)

Host Links

The whole crew is back for another roundtable, and things get wild right out of the gate. I sat down with Steve, Mechanic, and Jeff to talk through the absolute mess that is the current state of things. Jeff shares an update on his Canadian immigration purgatory, which spirals into a bigger conversation about living like an outlaw and whether any of us are truly free from the system.

But we couldn't ignore the recent news. We talk about the crazy story of a guy who used Claude to brute-force a lost Bitcoin wallet from eleven years ago. It really makes you rethink how safe your standard passphrases actually are, and we share some wild visual memory tricks to secure your setup. We also rip into the current hype around ETF inflows and outflows. People track these numbers like they are predicting the future, but we explain why they really just tell you exactly what the market already did.

And of course, we had to get into the technical fights that never seem to end. Steve brings some fresh data exposing the true bimodal distribution of network users right now. We spend a good amount of time arguing about Taproot, chain bloat, and the massive confusion between true censorship and simply stopping spam. Mechanic lays out his latest case for BIP 110 and makes a really compelling point about why the healthiest participants for the network are actually the ones willing to mine at a complete technical loss. It sounds completely backward, but it makes total sense once you hear his breakdown.

We wrap things up by looking at the broader picture of decentralised movements. From Occupy Wall Street falling apart to the current crop of hijacked political factions, we ask if it is even possible to build a movement without a vulnerable central leader. Plus, we drop some thoughts on local LLMs and the ongoing Prime Trust bankruptcy drama.

Guest Links

  • Steve Simple Nostr (Link: https://tinyurl.com/3s6a8yn8)
  • Steve Simple on X (Link: https://x.com/stevesimple)
  • Bitcoin Mechanic Nostr (Link: https://tinyurl.com/2tm827ut)
  • Bitcoin Mechanic on X (Link: https://x.com/GrassFedBitcoin)
  • Jeff Swann Nostr (Link: https://tinyurl.com/3sjc3bcp)

item.title: State of Bitcoin with Brandon Keys – The European Bitcoin Ban Hiding In Plain Sight with Matyas Kuchar - State of Bitcoin Ep. 238
item.description:
Until last year, more than 1,000 restaurants in Czech Republic accepted Bitcoin. Then it all stopped.

The MiCA regulation didn't ban Bitcoin in Europe. It did something far more effective — it made the rails impossible to operate. And as the Czech merchant economy quietly disappeared, two bigger forces started moving in the background: the petrodollar dying, and CBDCs coming online in Europe THIS YEAR.

In this episode, I sit down with Matyas Kuchar — co-founder of BTC Prague, the largest Bitcoin-only conference in Europe, and one of the most thoughtful voices on European Bitcoin policy — to break down why Czech Republic became the Bitcoin capital of Europe, why the European surveillance state is spreading faster than Americans realize, and why he believes Bitcoin must win because the alternative is just so much worse.

You'll learn:

→ How MiCA quietly killed 1,000+ Bitcoin-accepting restaurants in Czech Republic — and why it wasn't even a direct ban

→ Why Czech Republic became the Bitcoin capital of Europe (Trezor, Slush Pool, BIP-39, General Bytes — all started in Prague)

→ Why the petrodollar is dying — and what the loss of US hegemony means for global decentralization

→ Why Europeans use Bitcoin as money while Americans treat it as digital gold — and which approach is right

→ The European CBDC threat: pilots are launching THIS YEAR — and why most Americans haven't heard about it

→ The CBDC mechanism in Matyas's words: "You can analyze, you can control, you can surveil, you can send from a helicopter"

→ Why even an anarcho-capitalist now meets with politicians — and what changed his mind

→ Why Bitcoin is no longer just savings — it's the network of people building the future you actually want to live in

🚨 JOIN ME AT BTC PRAGUE 2026

The largest Bitcoin-only conference in Europe — June 11-13. Use code GREENCANDLE for discounted tickets:

https://btcprague.com/

🎯 PROTECT YOUR BITCOIN WITH SALT LENDING

Borrow against your Bitcoin without selling a single sat. Green Candle viewers get an exclusive offer:

https://saltlending.com/green-candle

🎯 PLAN YOUR WEALTH WITH STRONG WEALTH

Build a Bitcoin-aligned wealth strategy — estate planning, tax optimization, portfolio construction:

https://strongwealth.net/

🎯 MINE BITCOIN WITH SIMPLE MINING

First-year repairs free for Green Candle listeners:

https://simplemining.io/green-candle

📊 REFERENCED IN THIS EPISODE

• MiCA regulation (EU 2023/1114) — Markets in Crypto-Assets framework

• Czech Republic Bitcoin tax exemption (3-year hold rule)

• Slush Pool (founded 2010 in Prague, first ever Bitcoin mining pool)

• BIP-39 seed phrase standard (developed in Prague)

• Trezor hardware wallet (first ever, founded in Prague)

• EU CBDC pilot programs (launching 2026)

• BTC Prague 2026 conference — June 11-13

Bitcoin #BTCPrague #Matyas #MiCA #BitcoinEurope #CBDC #BitcoinPolicy #EuropeanBitcoin #BitcoinConference #SoundMoney #Petrodollar #BitcoinAdoption #CzechRepublic #FinancialFreedom #BitcoinNews

🔔 SUBSCRIBE for weekly Bitcoin, macro, and personal finance content.

⚠️ Disclaimer: This video is for informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Always do your own research.

]]>

item.title: BitBucks Bitcoin Podcast – Bitcoin und Deutschland: Wo stehen wir und wo kann die Reise hingehen? #6 mit Larry
item.description:
Herzlich willkommen zum BitBucks Bitcoin Podcast(https://www.bitbucks.de)!

Heute haben wir einen besonderen Gast: Larry, bekannt als @B1tcoin3r auf X. Larry ist ein erfahrener Bitcoin-Experte, Vortragsredner und Workshop-Host. Dank seines weitreichenden Netzwerks in führenden Positionen bringt er tiefgehende Einblicke in die aktuelle Bitcoin-Situation in Deutschland mit. Gemeinsam sprechen wir über die deutsche Bitcoin-Politik, die Auswirkungen der neuen Regierung und die mögliche Zukunft von Bitcoin in Deutschland.

Viel Spaß beim Zuhören!

Zudem reden wir auch noch über die Themen:

- Die aktuelle Bitcoin Lage in Deutschland (Adaption, Beliebtheit)

- Lösungen für eine Bitcoin Adaption

- Einfluss der Politik auf Bitcoin

- Ob Bitcoin die Politik überhaupt braucht- Bitcoin und Unternehmen bzw. Start-Ups

- Wie viele Jahre es dauern wird, bis Deutschland Bitcoin als offizielles Zahlungsmittel einführt

- Bitcoin Städte Spiel: Errate die fünf beliebtesten Bitcoin Städte weltweit

- Wie Bitcoin Larrys Leben verändert hat

- Glaskugel: Bitcoin Preis am Ende des Jahres 2025Podcast Folge auf Spotify:

---------------------------------------------------------------------

Google Play: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=de.fuf.bitbucks&pli=1

https://www.instagram.com/bit_bucks/

https://x.com/bit_bucksPodcast auf Spotify:

----------------------------------------------------------------------

Vernetz dich gerne mit den Rednern :)

LinkedIn Konsti Schwemlein: https://www.linkedin.com/in/konstantin-schwemlein/

X/Twitter Larry: https://x.com/B1tcoin3r

--------------------------------------------------------------

TimeStamps:

00:00 Intro

01:15 Wie bist du zu Bitcoin gekommen?

03:20 Wie steht es um Bitcoin in Deutschland?

07:45 Thema Bitcoin: Bevölkerung vs. Unternehmen/Politik

12:46 Was sind deine Verbesserungsvorschläge?

15:15 Wie kann die deutsche Politik Bitcoin integrieren?

17:28 Gleicht eine Bitcoin Reserve die Staatsschulden aus?

19:50 Wird eine CDU Regierung das Thema Bitcoin fördern?

item.title: Münzcrime - True Crime Bitcoin Podcast – Mt. Gox: "Withdrawal denied". Episode 5
item.description:
Doch der Weg ins Chaos war kein einziger Sprung. Es begann mit einer Serie von Sicherheitspannen und technischem Versagen – Warnsignale, die ignoriert wurden. Diese Inkompetenz schuf ein Vakuum, ein digitales Schlupfloch, durch das schließlich nicht nur Fehler, sondern echte Angreifer strömten. Erst die chronischen Pannen, dann der massive Hack, der das System endgültig zum Einsturz brachte.

Willkommen zur fünften Ausgabe unseres Podcasts. Wir sind tief in den Fall Mt. Gox eingetaucht, um diesen komplexen Skandal chronologisch zu entwirren. Von den bescheidenen Anfängen in der Frühzeit von Bitcoin bis hin zu den heutigen, kaum zu fassenden Auswüchsen dieses historischen Desasters. Wir zeigen euch, wie aus einer kleinen Nischenplattform einer der größten Bitcoin-Skandale aller Zeiten wurde – und warum die Folgen bis heute nachhallen.

V4V:
crime21@getalby.com

Nostr:
npub14cz50cvahflq8kpe57qxnnvgmveyng6l8fdtessdqm8ht9fcly3qjd20ry

X:
https://x.com/Muenzcrime

E-Mail:
muenzcrime@tuta.io

Links zur Folge:
https://www.mtgox.com/ 
https://forklog.com/en/mt-gox-the-biggest-hack-in-cryptocurrency-history/?keywords=binance&sort=-relevance&index&is_active=true 
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mt._Gox 
https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2015/02/19/report-mt-gox-data-provides-more-clues-to-trading-bot-willy 
https://willyreport.wordpress.com/2014/05/25/the-willy-report-proof-of-massive-fraudulent-trading-activity-at-mt-gox-and-how-it-has-affected-the-price-of-bitcoin/ 
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hvCdu0zsSP4 
https://x.com/brucefenton/status/2049593550506242127 
https://darknetdiaries.com/transcript/9/ 
https://www.irs.gov/zh-hans/compliance/criminal-investigation/russian-nationals-charged-with-hacking-one-cryptocurrency-exchange-and-illicitly-operating-another 
https://www.vccircle.com/former-ceo-of-failed-bitcoin-exchange-mt-gox-pleads-not-guilty-to-embezzlement 
https://bdnews24.com/world/d36b99f0b6f3 
https://www.cnbc.com/2024/07/05/mt-gox-begins-repaying-bitcoin-to-creditors-a-decade-on-from-collapse.html

https://arstechnica.com/tech-policy/2017/11/ceo-who-presided-over-mt-goxs-collapse-could-end-up-with-massive-profits/]]>

item.description:
In dieser Episode spricht Philipp mit Robin Seyr und Ben Raich im Auto auf dem Weg von San Salvador nach El Zonte über ihre Eindrücke aus El Salvador. Sie diskutieren die Bitcoin-Adoption, die Adopting Bitcoin-Konferenz, die Sicherheitslage, die alltägliche Nutzung von Bitcoin und die politische Situation unter Präsident Bukele.


Wer ist Robin Seyr?

Robin Seyr ist Host vom täglichen Bitcoin Podcast.


Wer ist Ben Raich?

Ben Raich arbeitet mit Leidenschaft an vielfältigen Projekten im Bitcoin-Space, um Bildung und Adaption von Bitcoin voranzutreiben. Sein Schwerpunkt liegt auf der Produktion hochwertiger Videopodcasts, dem Videoschnitt sowie journalistischen Tätigkeiten.

item.title: Bald in Bitcoin – Bald in Bitcoin - Folge 13: Die stille Steuer – Warum der Staat Inflation nicht bekämpfen will
item.description:
Deine Eltern haben dir gesagt: Leg Geld zurück. Sparen lohnt sich.

Guter Rat – damals. Aber heute ist etwas kaputt.

In Folge 12 haben wir gezeigt, warum dein Kontostand stabil aussehen kann und du trotzdem ärmer wirst. Heute gehen wir einen Schritt weiter und stellen die unbequeme Frage dahinter: Warum wird Inflation nicht einfach abgestellt?

Die Antwort: Weil das System genau so gebaut ist.

DAS LERNST DU IN DIESER FOLGE

– Warum Staaten gigantische Schulden durch Inflation heimlich entwerten – Was finanzielle Repression bedeutet – und warum das kein Verschwörungsmythos ist – Wer wirklich die Rechnung zahlt: die stille Steuer auf deine Ersparnisse – Warum das 2%-Ziel der EZB keine zufällige Zahl ist – Was Bitcoin als mengenmäßig begrenztes System damit zu tun hat

WEITERFÜHRENDE EMPFEHLUNGEN

Der Bitcoin-Standard – Saifedean Ammous Schulden: Die ersten 5000 Jahre – David Graeber Reinhart & Sbrancia: The Liquidation of Government Debt (Studie, englisch)

VOR-ORT-KURS – 30. MAI 2026

Bitcoin für Einsteiger – digitales Geld für analog Geborene. Kein Trading. Kein Hype. Einfach ein ruhiger, verständlicher Einstieg. https://bitucation.com/kurs/vor-ort-bitcoin

Hinweis: In dieser Folge wird neben meiner Stimme eine KI-generierte Expertenstimme für technische Erklärpassagen eingesetzt. Alle Inhalte werden von mir recherchiert und geprüft.

⚠️ Keine Finanzberatung. Ausschließlich Bildung und persönliche Perspektive.

]]>

item.title: 21Focus - Der Bitcoin-Unternehmer Podcast – Aus der Banken IT in die Welt von Bitcoin - Harald Betz
item.description:
Folge #61 – Ein Gespräch über Banken IT und Bitcoin Bücher

Harald Beetz ist seit fast 20 Jahren selbstständig in der Beratung für Banken und Rechenzentren. Angefangen als Banklehrling bei der Sparkasse, hat er früh den Wandel von Beratung hin zu reinem Vertrieb erlebt und sich auf IT-Projekte spezialisiert. In diesem Gespräch erzählt er, wie er von der Banken IT zu Bitcoin kam, warum er mit seiner Frau einen Roman über Geld schrieb und wie er sich heute zwischen Beratung und Content rund um Bitcoin positioniert.


Du erfährst,

  • wie Harald die Bankenwelt von innen erlebt hat,
  • welche Herausforderungen und Chancen es als IT-Berater gibt,
  • warum er Bitcoin zunächst wieder verkaufte und später mit völlig anderem Blick neu entdeckte,
  • wie aus einer Buchidee seiner Frau ein gemeinsames Projekt wurde
  • und welche unternehmerischen Lektionen er aus fast zwei Jahrzehnten Selbstständigkeit mitnimmt.


Kapitel:

  • 00:00 Begrüßung und Vorstellung
  • 02:15 Vom Banklehrling zum IT-Berater
  • 07:40 Veränderungen in der Bankenwelt seit den 2000er Jahren
  • 15:10 Einstieg in die Selbstständigkeit und erste Projekte
  • 22:05 Gescheiterte Produktidee und der Wechsel zur Beratung
  • 29:15 Erster Kontakt mit Bitcoin und die zweite Begegnung
  • 36:50 Der Roman über Geld und Bitcoin
  • 44:20 Zukunft der Beratung im Banken- und Bitcoin-Umfeld
  • 49:00 Wichtigste Tipps für Vertrieb und Resilienz


Links:

Youtube: youtube.com/@MiesesGeld

Webseite: miesesgeld.de

LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/haraldbetz


---

21 Focus - Der Bitcoin-Unternehmer Podcast

Hallo, mein Name ist Daniel Wingen und ich suche den Austausch mit anderen Unternehmern die in Bitcoin eine wichtiges Werkzeug für ihr Unternehmen sehen. Ich möchte selbst lernen und andere dazu inspirieren sich mit Bitcoin im eigenen Unternehmen zu beschäftigen.

Hier kannst Du Teil der Community werden und mich kontaktieren:

Telegram Community: ⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠t.me/B21Focus⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠ 

Daniel auf X: ⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠x.com/danielwingen⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠ 

Daniel auf LinkedIn:⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠⁠ linkedin.com/in/danielwingen/⁠

]]>

item.title: Bitcoin.com News Weekly Update – Bitcoin’s 20% Crash, Strategy Panic, and the New Crypto Security Threat
item.description:
Bitcoin fell roughly 20% in six days, triggering massive liquidations and renewed fear across the crypto market.

On this episode of The Weekly, David Sencil and Graham Stone unpack the crash, the panic around Strategy’s Bitcoin sale, and why traditional markets, AI stocks, semiconductors, and Fed expectations may be driving Bitcoin’s next move.

They also cover a major milestone for Bitcoin adoption: the first Bitcoin-backed home loan. Then the episode turns to Polymarket’s alleged undisclosed influencer payments and a disturbing rise in wrench attacks targeting crypto holders and their families.

A full breakdown of market chaos, Bitcoin utility, crypto controversy, and personal security in one high-impact episode.


Chapters:

01:27 - Market Sentiment and Liquidations

02:39 - Bitcoin Price Movements

04:06 - Strategy and Equity Market Concerns

05:25 - STRC and Market Confidence

07:20 - Global Market Reactions

09:25 - US Jobs Report and Market Impact

11:44 - Strategy's Future Moves

15:34 - Bitcoin-Backed Loans

17:26 - Crypto as Collateral

19:00 - Polymarket Investigation

20:39 - Crypto Crime and Security Concerns

23:39 - Global Crypto Attacks

26:48 - Data-Driven Targeting and Security

]]>

item.title: VERIFIED by Bitcoin Suisse – Can Bitcoin Escape the Quantum Black Hole?
item.description:
Is quantum computing Bitcoin's biggest hidden risk - and is anyone actually solving it? In this episode, Dominic Weibel and Luca Gnos sit down with Charles Edwards, founder of Capriole Investments, to break down how geopolitics, macro conditions, and quantum risk are shaping Bitcoin's next move. Edwards shares his onchain valuation tools pointing to a ~43% discount from fair value, explains why institutions now dominate Bitcoin's demand structure, and makes the case that quantum risk is already suppressing price - with a potential major repricing on the horizon within 12–24 months.

]]>

item.title: Thank God for Bitcoin – To The Unknown Pod Episode 42: Bitcoin and A World At War
item.description:
In this episode, Jordan Bush and Ryan Finlay explore the complex intersections of global conflict, economic warfare, and Bitcoin's role as a resilient alternative in a world at war. They analyze recent Middle East tensions, the decline of the petrodollar, and how Bitcoin offers a form of financial sovereignty amidst geopolitical chaos.

]]>

item.title: Epicenter - Learn about Crypto, Blockchain, Ethereum, Bitcoin and Distributed Technologies – Why Only 3 Builders Control All of Ethereum
item.description:
In this episode, host Friederike Ernst is joined by Kubi Mensah, CEO and co-founder of Gattaca, the company behind Titan Builder. Kubi sheds light on the highly competitive and often opaque world of Ethereum block building, explaining how Gattaca evolved from a centralized exchange proprietary trading firm to one of the three dominant builders responsible for constructing the vast majority of Ethereum blocks today .
 



 
They dissect the true "journey of a transaction," revealing why over half of all Ethereum transaction value bypasses the public mempool in favor of private order flow auctions and MEV-protection services . Kubi explains the intricate mechanics of top-of-block bidding by high-frequency DeFi arbitrageurs, the necessity of extreme latency optimization, and the "flywheel effect" that makes block building a natural oligopoly . Finally, the discussion turns to the future of the Ethereum roadmap, unpacking how upcoming upgrades like ePBS (enshrined Proposer-Builder Separation) and FOCIL (Fork-Choice Enforced Inclusion Lists) aim to permanently alter the power dynamics between block builders, validators, and originators .
 
 
Topics


  • 04:15 Transitioning from TradFi Trading to Ethereum Block Building
  • 09:30 Redefining the Builder: Relays and Order Flow Auctions
  • 15:00 Unpacking the Mempool: Public vs. Private Transactions
  • 21:45 The Anatomy of a Block & "Top-of-Block" Arbitrage
  • 27:10 How Builders Pay Proposers to Win Auctions
  • 35:20 The Oligopoly: Why Only 3 Builders Dominate Ethereum
  • 42:15 Trust in the Dark Forest: Handling Searcher Bundles
  • 49:00 The Convergence of Builders and Solvers
  • 55:30 The Impact of ePBS, FOCIL, and Pre-confirmations




Sponsors:



Lido V3 introduces stVaults: a modular staking infrastructure that lets builders and institutions deploy custom staking vaults, while staying anchored to stETH as a shared liquidity layer.Get started building with Lido V3 today: https://lido.fi/stvaults?mtm_campaign=epicenterBlock Space Forum: https://blockspace.forum/

]]>

  • 27:10 How Builders Pay Proposers to Win Auctions
  • 35:20 The Oligopoly: Why Only 3 Builders Dominate Ethereum
  • 42:15 Trust in the Dark Forest: Handling Searcher Bundles
  • 49:00 The Convergence of Builders and Solvers
  • 55:30 The Impact of ePBS, FOCIL, and Pre-confirmations




Sponsors:



Lido V3 introduces stVaults: a modular staking infrastructure that lets builders and institutions deploy custom staking vaults, while staying anchored to stETH as a shared liquidity layer.Get started building with Lido V3 today: https://lido.fi/stvaults?mtm_campaign=epicenterBlock Space Forum: https://blockspace.forum/

item.title: Der Relai Bitcoin Podcast – Bitcoins Einfluss auf globale Kapitalmärkte, mit Leon Wankum | Relai Bitcoin Podcast #123
item.description:
Leon Wankum ist der Head of Bitcoin Strategies bei oneVest, einem Familienunternehmen mit Fokus auf Immobilienentwicklung und Investments. Leon ist jedoch auch aktiv in der Bitcoin-Community und ist regelmässig in Podcasts zu hören oder schreibt seinen eigenen Newsletter. Nun hat er auch sein eigenes Bitcoin-Buch veröffentlicht.

Im Gespräch mit Relai-CEO Julian Liniger erzählt Leon von seiner Motivation, ein Bitcoin-Buch zu schreiben, weshalb ihn Bitcoin nach über 10 Jahren immer noch so motiviert, wie er die Lage rund um Bitcoin auf der Bilanz sieht, und wohin Bitcoin in den kommenden Jahren noch gehen wird.

Folge Leon auf X: https://x.com/leonwankum

Bestelle sein Buch hier: https://store.bitcoinmagazine.com/pages/digital-real-estate-pre-order

Oder besuche seine Webseite: https://leonwankum.com/

—

⏱ Kapitel:

00:00 - Intro und Vorstellung

00:50 - Bitcoin und digitales Immobilieninvestment

03:28 - Bitcoin als Grundlage für Wohlstand

09:25 - Metaplanet und Bitcoin als Kapital

12:14 - Werbeunterbrechung für die Relai-App

16:45 - Strategy’s neue Finanzinstrumente

23:50 - Bitcoin im globalen Finanzsystem

34:10 - Abschluss und Ausblick

—

Willst du dir die Sendung lieber anhören?

🎧Auf Apple Podcast anhören:

https://podcasts.apple.com/at/podcast/der-relai-bitcoin-podcast/id1543240691

🎧Auf Spotify anhören:

https://open.spotify.com/show/4nwQa2HLv2d9GHnGm6GpoO?si=2bd070927706437d

—

Die in der Sendung geäusserten Meinungen sind die Meinungen der einzelnen Gäste und geben nicht unbedingt die Meinung der Relai AG wieder. Die Informationen im Podcast können ohne Vorankündigung geändert werden, und es wird nicht garantiert, dass sie richtig, vollständig oder aktuell sind.

—

🎓 Lerne Bitcoin besser kennen:

🧠 Kostenloser Bitcoin-Kurs: https://relai.app/de/21-bitcoin-basics/

📥 Kostenloser wöchentlicher Bitcoin-Newsletter: https://relai.app/de/newsletter

👀 Brauchst du Hilfe? Stell uns eine Frage: https://support.relai.app/de

Folge uns:

Instagram: https://instagram.com/relai.app

Twitter/X: https://twitter.com/relai_app

LinkedIn: https://linkedin.com/company/relai

Haftungsausschluss: Relai ist gemäss dem MiCA-Regulierungsrahmen berechtigt, Krypto-Asset-Dienstleistungen in der Schweiz und in der gesamten Europäischen Union anzubieten. Nach Abschluss der Passporting-Verfahren baut das Unternehmen seine Dienstleistungen aktiv auf weitere EU-Mitgliedstaaten aus.

Bitcoin #Finanzen #Investieren #RelaiApp #SwissBitcoin #StackSats #21BitcoinBasics #BitcoinWissen

item.title: BitcoinTina on Bitcoin - Bitcoin Magazine – The Hardest Trade Part II
item.description:
CK, Bitcoin Tina, and Joe Carlasare are back with the sixth installment in the Bitcoin Tina on Bitcoin series! In this podcast, this epic cast talks about what it means to be the "buyer of last resort" and how to navigate the volatility of the Bitcoin Markets now and in the coming years. 

Connect with us on social media and join the conversation:

Twitter: https://twitter.com/BitcoinMagazine

item.title: The Bitcoin Source: A Bitcoin Conversation – UNPACKING THE UNBANKED FILM w/ Lauren Sieckmann
item.description:
In this raw conversation, Lauren Sieckmann unpacks the Unbanked documentary. The film unlocks Bitcoin as escape hatch for the billions locked out of banks. Real stories from inflation-crushed economies. The grind of four years filming across continents.
